steps.title: Set up the conditional probability
steps.content:
The phrase given that the participant is at least 10 years of age restricts us to the 10–19 years and 20+ years columns only. We need:
$$P(\text{Group A} \mid \text{at least 10 years}) = \frac{\text{# in Group A AND at least 10 years}}{\text{# at least 10 years}}$$

steps.title: Extract the values from the table
steps.content:
• Participants at least 10 years old: $40 + 40 = 80$ (sum of the 10–19 and 20+ columns)
• Group A participants at least 10 years old: $18 + 7 = 25$ (reading Group A row, 10–19 and 20+ columns)

steps.title: Calculate the probability
steps.content:
$$P(\text{Group A} \mid \text{at least 10 years}) = \frac{25}{80} = \frac{5}{16}$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
